tasks.title: Rename freezeâ†’stall in liveness detector and Frozenâ†’Benched in scheduler
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 9.1â€“9.4.
Three systems currently use "freeze" naming. This lap renames two of them for clarity; the third (per-agent-type `frozen` in `agent_status.jsonl`) keeps its name.

## Outcome
- `internal/reliability/freeze.go` renamed to `stall.go`; `FreezeDetector`â†’`StallDetector`, `Assessment.Frozen`â†’`Assessment.Stalled`, all threshold/field names updated.
- `internal/routing/scheduler.go`: `EntryState.Frozen`â†’`EntryState.Benched`; `failureFreezesEntry`â†’`failureBenchesEntry` with an explicit enum/boolean parameter on `OnAgentFailed` (replace `strings.Contains(reason, "freeze")` string-matching).
- `internal/relay/resilience.go`: `RecordHourlyFailure` counting loop also breaks on `frozen` and `probation` events, not just `active` (task 9.4).
- All callers in `internal/relay/runner.go`, `internal/relay/route_runtime.go`, and tests updated.
- The per-agent-type `frozen` name and `agent_status.jsonl` `event_type` value stay unchanged (task 9.3 â€” no data-format change).

## Files & scope
- `internal/reliability/freeze.go` â†’ `stall.go` (rename file + all exported names)
- `internal/reliability/freeze_test.go` â†’ `stall_test.go`
- `internal/routing/scheduler.go` + `scheduler_test.go`
- `internal/relay/runner.go`, `route_runtime.go` â€” update callers only
- `internal/relay/resilience.go` â€” task 9.4 counting-loop fix
- Do NOT touch `agent_status.jsonl` event_type values or the per-agent-type `frozen` name in resilience.go.

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- All existing tests pass (`go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/routing/... ./internal/relay/...`)
- `grep -r "FreezeDetector\|Assessment\.Frozen\|EntryState\.Frozen\|failureFreezesEntry" internal/` returns zero hits
- `grep -r "StallDetector\|Assessment\.Stalled\|EntryState\.Benched\|failureBenchesEntry" internal/` returns expected hits
- Mark off tasks 9.1, 9.2, 9.3, 9.4 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: Extend ClassifyError with infra/agent/incomplete failure classes
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 2.1, 5.1, 5.6.
Currently `ClassifyError` has basic classification. This lap adds the three-class distinction that everything else depends on.

## Outcome
- `internal/reliability/patterns.go` exports a `FailureClass` enum: `FailureInfra`, `FailureAgent`, `FailureIncomplete`.
- `ClassifyError` extended with patterns for:
  - **infra**: rate-limit keywords, harness/launch errors (`argument list too long`, `fork/exec`), API timeout/network stall patterns, stall-detection signals.
  - **agent**: ordinary agent errors, short no-op markers.
  - **incomplete**: new class (caller will set this based on working-tree state, not error text â€” but the type must be defined here).
- Unknown failures default to `FailureAgent` (task 5.6 â€” does-not-freeze side).
- Tests cover each pattern family and the unknown-defaults-to-agent behavior.

## Design
- `FailureIncomplete` is special: it cannot be determined from error text alone. The classification function should accept an optional context struct (e.g. `ClassifyContext{HasFileChanges bool, Finalized bool}`) or the caller sets it post-hoc. Choose whichever is cleaner â€” the key contract is that `FailureIncomplete` is a first-class member of the enum.
- Keep patterns testable: the pattern table should be a var or const slice, not buried in if/else chains.

## Acceptance
- `go test ./internal/reliability/...` passes with new tests
- Each infra pattern (rate-limit, fork/exec, argument list too long, timeout, stall) has at least one test case
- Unknown/unmatched errors return `FailureAgent`
- Mark off tasks 2.1, 5.1, 5.6 in the change tasks.md

## Outcome

tasks.title: Add ResilienceKey type and refactor all resilience methods to per-harness-model keying
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 5.2, 5.3, 5.4.
Currently all resilience methods key on `agentType string`. This lap refactors to `ResilienceKey{Harness, Model}` so rate-limit/freeze state is per harness-model pair.

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/resilience.go`: define `ResilienceKey{Harness, Model string}` type. Update all method signatures: `getState(key)`, `PauseAgent(key, relayID)`, `RecordHourlyFailure(key, relayID)`, `FreezeAgent(key, relayID)`, `UnpauseAgent(key, relayID)`, `SelectActiveAgent(mix, runIndex)` â€” keying on `harness:model`.
- `internal/store/store.go` / `records.go`: add optional `model` field to `AgentStatusEvent`; update `GetAgentStatus` to accept a model parameter and filter on both `agent_type` and `model` when model is present.
- All callers in `internal/relay/runner.go` (~20+ call sites) and `internal/relay/route_runtime.go` updated to construct `ResilienceKey` from `selection.Agent.Model` (or resolved model).
- Existing tests updated to pass `ResilienceKey` instead of plain strings.

## Design
- The `ResilienceKey` type should have a `String()` method returning `harness:model` for logging/display.
- `SelectActiveAgent` uniqueness map keys change from `string` to `ResilienceKey`.
- `GetAgentStatus(agentType, model)` â€” when model is empty string, filter on `agent_type` only (backward compat).
- This is a wide-but-mechanical refactor. Enumerate all 10 method signatures and confirm all callers at the end.

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- All existing tests pass (with updated keying)
- `grep -rn "func.*PauseAgent\|func.*FreezeAgent\|func.*getState\|func.*RecordHourlyFailure\|func.*UnpauseAgent\|func.*SelectActiveAgent" internal/relay/` shows all signatures take `ResilienceKey`
- Mark off tasks 5.2, 5.3, 5.4 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: VERIFY: per-harness-model keying refactor
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle`. Verifying the ResilienceKey refactor (tasks 5.2, 5.3, 5.4). This was a wide refactor across ~10 method signatures and 20+ call sites â€” high risk.

## Outcome
- Confirm every resilience method signature takes `ResilienceKey`, not bare `string`
- Confirm `AgentStatusEvent` has `model` field and `GetAgentStatus` filters on both `agent_type` and `model`

- `go build ./...` passes
- All tests pass: `go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/store/...`

- Report findings; create head laps for substantive gaps

tasks.title: Add baseline tests for resilience.go
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle`.
`internal/relay/resilience.go` currently has NO dedicated test file â€” all testing is indirect through `runner_test.go` (117KB). The probation state machine (next laps) will heavily modify resilience.go, and we need a safety net of baseline tests before that work begins.

## Outcome
- Create `internal/relay/resilience_test.go` with tests covering the current behavior:
  - `getState` returns `StateActive` for an agent with no events
  - `getState` returns `StatePaused` after a pause event
  - `getState` returns `StateFrozen` after a freeze event
  - `PauseAgent` writes a paused event and `getState` reflects it
  - `UnpauseAgent` restores to active
  - `FreezeAgent` writes a frozen event
  - `RecordHourlyFailure` counts retry_failed events and auto-freezes after threshold
  - `RecordHourlyFailure` counting loop breaks correctly at state boundaries
  - `SelectActiveAgent` skips paused and frozen agents, cycles through active ones
- Use a test store (in-memory or temp dir) and `NowFunc` for time control.

## Design
- These tests should be straightforward unit tests â€” no integration with runner.
- Test the public API surface plus `getState` (even if unexported, test via exported methods if needed).
- Keep tests focused and independent so probation additions can be appended cleanly.

## Acceptance
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestResilience -v` passes
- Each state transition (activeâ†’paused, pausedâ†’active, pausedâ†’frozen, frozen stays frozen) has at least one test
- `RecordHourlyFailure` threshold behavior tested
- `SelectActiveAgent` cycling tested

tasks.title: Add probation state machine, resilience constants, and agent status window bump
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 3.1â€“3.7.
The resilience state machine currently has Active/Paused/Frozen with no decay. This lap adds `StateProbation` and makes freeze recoverable.

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/constants.go` (new file): centralize `FreezeDuration` (5h), `PauseDuration` (1h), `HourlyRetriesBeforeFreeze` (5), `HourlyRetryMaxAttempts` (3). Replace any existing hardcoded values in `resilience.go` and `runner.go`.
- `internal/relay/resilience.go`: add `StateProbation` to the state enum. `getState(key)` returns `StateProbation` when a frozen event is older than `FreezeDuration`; remains a pure read function (no side effects, no event writes).
- `internal/relay/route_runtime.go` `syncRecoverySignals`: implement one-shot enforcement â€” unbench the scheduler entry for probation, then immediately re-bench it so the same entry cannot be re-selected. When the run resolves, the new state (active or frozen) is reflected back. Persist probation event (`event_type: "probation"`) exactly once when first observing the frozenâ†’probation transition.
- `internal/relay/runner.go` `runOne`: probation runs get `maxAttempts=3`; success or incomplete â†’ promote to active; failure (agent or infra) â†’ re-freeze with fresh timestamp.
- `internal/store/store.go`: bump `agentStatusWindowSize` from 50 to 500. On truncation, synthesize a summary event preserving the latest effective state + timestamp for any active frozen/probation entries.

## Design
- `getState` must NOT write events. Only `syncRecoverySignals` persists the probation event.
- The probation persistence check: scan recent events for a `probation` event with the same key after the last `frozen` event. If found, skip writing.
- Window truncation summary: when truncating, scan the about-to-be-dropped events for the latest frozen/probation per key, then prepend synthesized summary events to the retained window.

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- All existing tests pass
- New tests (task 3.8): frozen decays to probation after FreezeDuration; probation one-shot enforcement in syncRecoverySignals; probation success â†’ active; probation incomplete â†’ active; probation failure â†’ re-frozen; all-frozen ends pass but remains decayable; window truncation preserves freeze timestamps
- `go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/store/...` passes
- Mark off tasks 3.1â€“3.7 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: Tests for probation state machine
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” task 3.8.
The probation state machine is the highest-risk piece of this change. This dedicated lap ensures thorough test coverage.

## Outcome
- Dedicated test file or section in `internal/relay/resilience_test.go` (create if needed) covering:
  - Frozen state decays to probation after `FreezeDuration` window
  - Probation one-shot enforcement in `syncRecoverySignals` (unbench â†’ immediately re-bench)
  - Probation success â†’ promoted to active
  - Probation incomplete â†’ promoted to active (incomplete is progress, not availability)
  - Probation failure â†’ re-frozen with fresh timestamp (decay window restarts)
  - All agents frozen: relay ends pass but freeze remains decayable for future starts
  - Window truncation at 500 events preserves freeze timestamps via synthesized summary events
  - `getState` is pure (no side effects, no event writes)
  - Probation event persisted exactly once per transition (not duplicated on re-evaluation)

## Design
- Use time-control (mock clock / `NowFunc`) to test freeze decay without real waits.
- Test the `syncRecoverySignals` scheduler interaction by checking entry state before/after.
- Integration-style tests that cover the full `runOne` â†’ probation â†’ promote/re-freeze flow are ideal.

## Acceptance
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestProbation -v` passes
- `go test ./internal/store/... -run TestWindow -v` covers truncation
- All existing tests still pass
- Mark off task 3.8 in the change tasks.md

## Outcome

tasks.title: Implement lap-ID pinning and attempted-lap recording
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 1.1â€“1.5.
The primary state-integrity guard: prevent phantom and multi-lap completions.

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/runner.go` / `RunState` (or equivalent): pin the assigned lap ID at run start. On finalization (`laps wrapup` / run end), compare recorded-completed laps against the pinned ID.
- Mismatch â†’ fail the run with `wrong_lap_consumed` (different lap) or `multi_lap_consumed` (>1 lap completed), and do NOT advance the queue.
- `internal/store/records.go` `TryRecord`: add `laps_attempted` field â€” a list of `{lap_id, timestamp}` recording every lap completion attempt.
- Tests: phantom-completion rejection (completed lap â‰  pinned lap), multi-lap rejection (>1 lap completed), normal single-lap pass-through, attempted-lap recording on try records.

## Design
- The pinning check is detection+containment, not prevention. The external laps queue may already be mutated by the time rally detects the mismatch. The important thing is that rally does NOT silently proceed.
- Log the pinned and consumed lap IDs on mismatch for manual/VERIFY recovery.
- `laps_attempted` is appended to for every `laps done`/`laps handoff` call observed, not just successful ones.

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestLapPin -v` passes
- Tests cover: wrong-lap rejection, multi-lap rejection, normal pass-through, attempted-lap field populated
- Mark off tasks 1.1â€“1.5 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: Implement incomplete failure class behavior and failure cascade gating
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 2.2â€“2.6, 5.5.
The incomplete class and >1 infra threshold both modify how `runner.go` handles try outcomes. Bundled because they share the same code path.

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/runner.go`:
  - When a try produces file changes (dirty working tree) but the agent did not finalize (no `laps done`/`laps handoff`): classify as incomplete.
  - Suppress auto-commit for incomplete tries â€” leave changes uncommitted so the retry agent inherits them as partial progress.
  - On retry after incomplete: inject prompt guidance: "The last run was incomplete. Check any current git changes, finish anything not done, verify correctness, commit when good, then run `laps done`."
  - Incomplete runs retry but do NOT call `PauseAgent`/`RecordHourlyFailure` (do not advance the resilience cascade).
  - Only call `PauseAgent`/`RecordHourlyFailure` when >1 attempt within a run is classified as infra-class. A single infra failure retries without escalation. Agent-class and incomplete failures stay retry-eligible but do not escalate.
- Tests:
  - Incomplete run leaves changes uncommitted
  - Retry agent gets incomplete guidance in prompt
  - Incomplete does not count toward pause/freeze cascade
  - >1 infra failure increments cascade
  - Single infra failure does not increment cascade
  - Agent error and incomplete do not increment cascade

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestIncomplete -v` and `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestFailureCascade -v` pass
- Mark off tasks 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 5.5 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: Hourly retry budget, --new agent reset, and per-harness-model isolation tests
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 6.1, 6.2, 4.1â€“4.3, 5.7.
Three bounded, pattern-following changes that depend on the foundation from earlier laps.

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/runner.go`: set `maxAttempts=3` on the hourly retry path (the `isHourlyRetry` path). Currently it is 1.
- `internal/store/store.go`: add `ResetAgentStatus()` that truncates agent status history for a clean slate.
- `cmd/rally/main.go`: route `--new` through `ResetAgentStatus()` so all harness-model pairs start active.
- Tests:
  - A single transient failure during an hourly retry does not burn a freeze life; retry budget of 3 honored (tasks 6.1, 6.2)
  - `--new` starts all agents active regardless of prior frozen/probation/paused history (task 4.3)
  - >1 infra failure increments cascade; single infra failure does not; agent error and incomplete do not; per-harness-model keying isolates rate-limit to specific model (task 5.7)

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/store/... ./cmd/rally/...` passes
- Mark off tasks 6.1, 6.2, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 5.7 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: VERIFY: lap pinning, incomplete class, failure gating, hourly retry, and --new reset
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle`. Verifying the core runner behavior changes: tasks 1.1â€“1.5, 2.2â€“2.6, 5.5, 5.7, 6.1â€“6.2, 4.1â€“4.3.

## Outcome
- Confirm lap-ID pinning works: wrong-lap and multi-lap mismatch fails the run, normal single-lap passes through, `laps_attempted` recorded on try records.
- Confirm incomplete classification: auto-commit suppressed, retry gets guidance prompt, incomplete does NOT escalate cascade.
- Confirm >1 infra threshold: single infra failure does not pause, >1 does.
- Confirm hourly retry budget is 3.
- Confirm `--new` calls `ResetAgentStatus()` and all agents start active.
- Confirm per-harness-model keying isolates rate-limit to specific model.
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./...` passes
- Use `openspec-verify-change` skill focused on these tasks
- Report findings; create head laps for substantive gaps

tasks.title: Implement role-aware stall-recovery (VERIFY excluded from files-committed recovery)
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 7.1â€“7.2.
The "files committed â†’ success" stall-recovery is unsafe for VERIFY runs: a VERIFY agent may legitimately commit only a trivial fix, which is not evidence that verification actually happened. This lap gates that recovery on role. No verdict artifact is introduced â€” that approach was considered and rejected (see design Decision 7).

## Outcome
- `internal/relay/runner.go`: gate the stall-recovery logic on role. When a try is killed by the stall detector:
  - VERIFY: NOT auto-accepted on the basis of committed files â€” the try stays a retry-eligible failure and is retried/resumed.
  - Implementation roles (junior/senior/ui): keep existing files-committed recovery.
- Tests:
  - Stalled VERIFY (even with a committed trivial fix) stays a retry-eligible failure
  - Stalled implementation try with commits still recovers

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestStallRecovery -v` passes
- Mark off tasks 7.1, 7.2 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: Add bounded prompt context with configurable budgets
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` â€” tasks 8.1â€“8.3.
The assembled prompt can grow unbounded because recent-try summaries are concatenated in full. This lap adds budgets.

## Outcome
- Config: add `recent_try_count` (default 5), `recent_try_char_limit` (per-summary char limit), `recent_context_char_limit` (overall char limit) under `[reliability]` in config.toml schema.
- `internal/relay/runner.go` (around line ~581 where `recentContext` is built): apply count + per-summary + overall character budgets. Truncation strategy: head/tail (keep first N and last N chars of each summary, with "... [truncated] ..." marker).
- Tests:
  - Verbose summaries truncated to per-summary limit
  - Count of recent tries honored (default 5)
  - Overall budget enforced
  - Short summaries pass through unchanged

## Files & scope
- `internal/relay/runner.go` â€” prompt assembly section
- Rally config struct (wherever `[reliability]` is parsed) â€” add fields
- Tests in `runner_test.go`

## Acceptance
- `go build ./...` passes
- `go test ./internal/relay/... -run TestPromptBudget -v` passes
- Mark off tasks 8.1, 8.2, 8.3 in the change tasks.md

tasks.title: VERIFY: role-aware stall-recovery and bounded prompt
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle`. Verifying tasks 7.1â€“7.2 (role-aware stall-recovery) and 8.1â€“8.3 (bounded prompt context).

## Outcome

- Confirm prompt context is bounded: count, per-summary, and overall budgets enforced

- Use `openspec-verify-change` skill focused on tasks 7.1â€“7.2, 8.1â€“8.3

tasks.description:

## Outcome

- Update `AGENTS.md`/role-doc references if stall-recovery behavior is documented there (task 10.1).

## Files & scope

## Acceptance

tasks.title: VERIFY: full change â€” final verification
tasks.description:
## Context
OpenSpec change: `harden-relay-run-lifecycle`. Final full-change verification covering the complete outcome.

## Outcome
- Run full OpenSpec verification: use `openspec-verify-change` skill against `harden-relay-run-lifecycle` to verify all tasks are marked done and implementation matches specs.
- Identify the branch target/base before diffing; use `git diff <target>...HEAD` to inspect the whole diff.
- Identify the first lap/try in the current batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing baseline.
- Full test suite: `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`
- Confirm:
  - No stale naming (FreezeDetector, Assessment.Frozen, EntryState.Frozen, failureFreezesEntry)
  - All resilience methods use ResilienceKey
  - Probation state machine correct (getState pure, one-shot enforcement, decay/re-freeze)
  - Lap-ID pinning works
  - Incomplete class behavior correct (no auto-commit, guidance prompt, no cascade)
  - >1 infra threshold enforced
  - Hourly retry budget is 3
  - --new resets agent status
  - Stall-recovery role-gated (VERIFY excluded from files-committed recovery)
  - Prompt context bounded
  - VERSION bumped
  - Docs updated
- Do NOT rewrite git history. If cleanup is needed, create focused head laps.
- Report findings with pass/fail verdict and summary.
